summary: Eculizumab is not approved or supported by current evidence for treating myasthenia gravis; it has no established role in this condition despite being effective in other immune-mediated disorders like paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria. The absence of therapeutic indication links directly to lack of documented efficacy or safety trials specifically within myasthenic patients.
